CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_BEGIN_UDF(PERIOD_VAL VARCHAR(22))
RETURNS TIMESTAMP
AS
' CAST(SUBSTR(PERIOD_VAL,1, POSITION(''*'',PERIOD_VAL)-1) AS TIMESTAMP) '     
;

Comment: Second argument of calendar type is optional in Teradata and will use the session default.  Tool needs to be able to specify a default for input into the code when the argument has been ommitted.

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.DAYNUMBER_OF_CALENDAR_UDF(INPUT_DT TIMESTAMP_LTZ, CAL_TYPE VARCHAR(20))
RETURNS INTEGER 
AS
$$
SELECT DAY_OF_CALENDAR FROM SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.TDBUSINESSCALENDAR WHERE CALENDAR_TYPE = CAL_TYPE AND CALENDAR_DATE = DATE_TRUNC(DAY,INPUT_DT)
$$
;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.DAYOCCURRENCE_OF_MONTH_UDF(INPUT_DT TIMESTAMP_LTZ, CAL_TYPE VARCHAR(20))
RETURNS INTEGER 
AS
$$
SELECT CAST(CASE MOD(DAY_OF_MONTH, 7) WHEN 0 THEN DAY_OF_MONTH/7 ELSE TRUNC(DAY_OF_MONTH/7+1) END AS INTEGER) FROM TDBUSINESSCALENDAR WHERE CALENDAR_TYPE = CAL_TYPE AND CALENDAR_DATE = DATE_TRUNC(DAY,INPUT_DT)
$$
;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_END_UDF(PERIOD_VAL VARCHAR(22))
RETURNS TIMESTAMP
AS
' CAST(SUBSTR(PERIOD_VAL,POSITION(''*'',PERIOD_VAL)+1) AS TIMESTAMP) '     
;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_LDIFF_UDF( PERIOD_1 VARCHAR(50), PERIOD_2 VARCHAR(50))
RETURNS VARCHAR
AS
' CASE WHEN SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_OVERLAPS_UDF(PERIOD_2, PERIOD_1) = ''TRUE'' 
            AND SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_BEGIN_UDF(PERIOD_1) < SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_BEGIN_UDF(PERIOD_2) 
       THEN
        SUBSTR(PERIOD_1,1, POSITION(''*'',PERIOD_1)-1) || ''*'' || SUBSTR(PERIOD_2,1, POSITION(''*'',PERIOD_2)-1)
       ELSE
         NULL
       END '     
;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.MONTHS_BETWEEN_UDF(INPUT_1 TIMESTAMP_LTZ, INPUT_2 TIMESTAMP_LTZ)
RETURNS NUMBER(20,6)
AS
$$
SELECT 
CASE WHEN DAY(INPUT_2) <= DAY(INPUT_1) 
           THEN TIMESTAMPDIFF(MONTH,INPUT_2,INPUT_1) 
            ELSE TIMESTAMPDIFF(MONTH,INPUT_2,INPUT_1) - 1 
END + 
(CASE 
    WHEN DAY(INPUT_2) = DAY(INPUT_1) THEN 0
    WHEN DAY(INPUT_2) < DAY(INPUT_1) AND TO_TIME(INPUT_2) > TO_TIME(INPUT_1) THEN DAY(INPUT_1) - DAY(INPUT_2) - 1
    WHEN DAY(INPUT_2) <= DAY(INPUT_1) THEN DAY(INPUT_1) - DAY(INPUT_2) 
    ELSE 31 - DAY(INPUT_2) + DAY(INPUT_1) 
 END / 31) +
(CASE 
    WHEN DAY(INPUT_2) = DAY(INPUT_1) THEN 0
    WHEN HOUR(INPUT_2) <= HOUR(INPUT_1) THEN HOUR(INPUT_1) - HOUR(INPUT_2) 
    ELSE 24 - HOUR(INPUT_2) + HOUR(INPUT_1) 
END / (24*31)) +   
(CASE 
     WHEN DAY(INPUT_2) = DAY(INPUT_1) THEN 0   
     WHEN MINUTE(INPUT_2) <= MINUTE(INPUT_1) THEN MINUTE(INPUT_1) - MINUTE(INPUT_2) 
     ELSE 24 - HOUR(INPUT_2) + MINUTE(INPUT_1) 
        END / (24*60*31)) +
(CASE 
     WHEN DAY(INPUT_2) = DAY(INPUT_1) THEN 0   
     WHEN MINUTE(INPUT_2) <= MINUTE(INPUT_1) THEN MINUTE(INPUT_1) - MINUTE(INPUT_2) 
     ELSE 24 - HOUR(INPUT_2) + MINUTE(INPUT_1) 
END / (24*60*60*31))
$$
;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_OVERLAPS_UDF(PERIOD_1 VARCHAR(22), PERIOD_2 VARCHAR(22))
RETURNS BOOLEAN 
AS
' CASE WHEN 
    (SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_END_UDF(PERIOD_1) >= SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_BEGIN_UDF(PERIOD_2) AND
     SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_BEGIN_UDF(PERIOD_1)  < SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_END_UDF(PERIOD_2))
    OR
    (SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_BEGIN_UDF(PERIOD_1) >= SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_BEGIN_UDF(PERIOD_2)AND
     SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_BEGIN_UDF(PERIOD_1) < SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_END_UDF(PERIOD_2)
    )
THEN
    TRUE
ELSE
    FALSE
END '
;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.RANDOM_UDF(low double, high double)
    RETURNS double
    language javascript
    AS
    $$
        return Math.floor(Math.random() * (HIGH - LOW + 1) + LOW);
    $$
    ;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_RDIFF_UDF( PERIOD_1 VARCHAR(50), PERIOD_2 VARCHAR(50))
RETURNS VARCHAR
AS
' CASE WHEN SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_OVERLAPS_UDF(PERIOD_2, PERIOD_1) = ''TRUE'' 
            AND SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_END_UDF(PERIOD_1) > SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.PERIOD_END_UDF(PERIOD_2) 
       THEN
        SUBSTR(PERIOD_2,POSITION(''*'',PERIOD_2)+1) || ''*'' || SUBSTR(PERIOD_1,POSITION(''*'',PERIOD_1)+1)
       ELSE
         NULL
       END '     
;

DocRef:
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.ROUND_DATE_UDF(INPUT TIMESTAMP_LTZ, FMT VARCHAR(5))
RETURNS DATE
AS
$$
SELECT
CAST(
CASE 
    WHEN 'FMT' IN ('CC','SCC') THEN 
        CASE 
            WHEN RIGHT(CAST(YEAR(INPUT) as CHAR(4)),2) >=51 
                THEN DATE_FROM_PARTS(CAST(LEFT(CAST(YEAR(INPUT) as CHAR(4)),2) || '01' as INTEGER) +100,1,1)
            ELSE DATE_FROM_PARTS(CAST(LEFT(CAST(YEAR(INPUT) as CHAR(4)),2) || '01' as INTEGER),1,1)
        END    
    WHEN 'FMT' IN ('SYYYY','YYYY','YEAR','SYEAR','YYY','YY','Y') THEN 
        CASE WHEN MONTH(INPUT) >= 7 THEN DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(CAST('2019-06-30' as DATE))+1,1,1)
             ELSE DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1)
        END
    WHEN 'FMT' IN ('IYYY','IYY','IY','I') THEN 
        CASE WHEN MONTH(CAST('2019-06-30' as DATE)) >= 7 THEN CASE DAYOFWEEK(DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),12,31))
                                                                  WHEN 0 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 1, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),12,31))
                                                                  WHEN 1 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 0, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),12,31))
                                                                  WHEN 2 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -1, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),12,31))
                                                                  WHEN 3 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -2, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),12,31))
                                                                  WHEN 4 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -3, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),12,31))
                                                                  WHEN 5 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 3, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),12,31))
                                                                  WHEN 6 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 2, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),12,31))
                                                              END
             ELSE CASE DAYOFWEEK(DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                      WHEN 0 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 1, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                      WHEN 1 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 0, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                      WHEN 2 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -1, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                      WHEN 3 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -2, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                      WHEN 4 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -3, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                      WHEN 5 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 3, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                      WHEN 6 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 2, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                  END  
        END
    WHEN 'FMT' IN ('MONTH','MON','MM','RM') THEN 
        CASE WHEN DAYOFMONTH(INPUT) >15 THEN TIMESTAMPADD(MONTH, 1, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),MONTH(INPUT),1))
             ELSE DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),MONTH(INPUT),1)
        END
    WHEN 'FMT' IN ('Q') THEN 
        CASE WHEN (MOD(MONTH(INPUT),3)=2 AND DAYOFMONTH(INPUT) >15) OR MOD(MONTH(INPUT),3)=0 
                THEN TIMESTAMPADD(MONTH, 3, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),(QUARTER(INPUT)-1)*3+1,1)) 
             ELSE DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),(QUARTER(INPUT)-1)*3+1,1)
        END
    WHEN 'FMT' IN ('WW') THEN 
        CASE WHEN MOD(TIMESTAMPDIFF(DAY,DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1),INPUT),7) < 4 
                THEN DATEADD(DAY, 0-MOD(TIMESTAMPDIFF(DAY,DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1),INPUT),7), INPUT)
             ELSE DATEADD(DAY, 7-MOD(TIMESTAMPDIFF(DAY,DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1),INPUT),7), INPUT)
        END
    WHEN 'FMT' IN ('IW') THEN 
        CASE WHEN MOD(TIMESTAMPDIFF(DAY,(CASE DAYOFWEEK(DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                             WHEN 0 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 1, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                             WHEN 1 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 0, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                             WHEN 2 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -1, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                             WHEN 3 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -2, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                             WHEN 4 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -3, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                             WHEN 5 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 3, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                             WHEN 6 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 2, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                         END
                   ), INPUT),7) >=4 THEN DATEADD(DAY,7-MOD(TIMESTAMPDIFF(DAY,(CASE DAYOFWEEK(DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                                                    WHEN 0 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 1, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                                                    WHEN 1 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 0, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                                                    WHEN 2 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -1, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                                                    WHEN 3 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -2, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                                                    WHEN 4 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -3, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                                                    WHEN 5 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 3, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                                                    WHEN 6 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 2, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                                                END), INPUT),7), INPUT)
             ELSE DATEADD(DAY,0-MOD(TIMESTAMPDIFF(DAY,(CASE DAYOFWEEK(DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                           WHEN 0 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 1, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                           WHEN 1 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 0, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                           WHEN 2 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -1, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                           WHEN 3 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -2, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                           WHEN 4 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -3, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                           WHEN 5 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 3, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                           WHEN 6 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 2, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                       END), INPUT),7), INPUT)
        END
    WHEN 'FMT' IN ('W') THEN 
        CASE WHEN MOD(TIMESTAMPDIFF(DAY,DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),MONTH(INPUT),1),INPUT),7) < 4 
                THEN DATEADD(DAY, 0-MOD(TIMESTAMPDIFF(DAY,DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),MONTH(INPUT),1),INPUT),7), INPUT)
            ELSE DATEADD(DAY, 7-MOD(TIMESTAMPDIFF(DAY,DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),MONTH(INPUT),1),INPUT),7), INPUT)
        END
    WHEN 'FMT' IN ('DDD', 'DD','J') THEN INPUT
    WHEN 'FMT' IN ('DAY', 'DY','D') THEN 
        CASE WHEN DAYOFWEEK(INPUT) > 3 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 7-DAYOFWEEK(INPUT), INPUT)
             ELSE DATEADD(DAY, 0-DAYOFWEEK(INPUT), INPUT)
        END
    WHEN 'FMT' IN ('HH', 'HH12','HH24') THEN INPUT
    WHEN 'FMT' IN ('MI') THEN INPUT
END AS DATE)
$$;

Comment: Second argument of calendar type is optional in Teradata and will use the session default.  Tool needs to be able to specify a default for input into the code when the argument has been ommitted.

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.TD_FRIDAY_UDF(INPUT_DT TIMESTAMP_LTZ, CAL_TYPE VARCHAR(20))
RETURNS TIMESTAMP_LTZ
AS
$$
SELECT TIMESTAMP_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(CALENDAR_DATE), MONTH_OF_YEAR, DAY_OF_MONTH, HOUR(INPUT_DT), MINUTE(INPUT_DT), SECOND(INPUT_DT), EXTRACT(NANOSECOND FROM CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)) 
FROM TDBUSINESSCALENDAR WHERE DAY_OF_CALENDAR = 
    (SELECT DAY_OF_CALENDAR - (CASE DAY_OF_WEEK WHEN 7 THEN 1 ELSE DAY_OF_WEEK + 1 END) 
     FROM TDBUSINESSCALENDAR WHERE CALENDAR_TYPE = CAL_TYPE AND CALENDAR_DATE = DATE_TRUNC(DAY,INPUT_DT))
$$
;

Comment: Second argument of calendar type is optional in Teradata and will use the session default.  Tool needs to be able to specify a default for input into the code when the argument has been ommitted.
DocRef:
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.TD_MONDAY_UDF(INPUT_DT TIMESTAMP_LTZ, CAL_TYPE VARCHAR(20))
RETURNS TIMESTAMP
AS
$$
SELECT TIMESTAMP_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(CALENDAR_DATE), MONTH_OF_YEAR, DAY_OF_MONTH, HOUR(INPUT_DT), MINUTE(INPUT_DT), SECOND(INPUT_DT), EXTRACT(NANOSECOND FROM CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)) 
FROM TDBUSINESSCALENDAR WHERE DAY_OF_CALENDAR = 
    (SELECT DAY_OF_CALENDAR - (CASE DAY_OF_WEEK WHEN 1 THEN 6 WHEN 2 THEN 7 ELSE DAY_OF_WEEK - 2 END)
     FROM TDBUSINESSCALENDAR WHERE CALENDAR_TYPE = CAL_TYPE AND CALENDAR_DATE = DATE_TRUNC(DAY,INPUT_DT))
$$
;

Comment: Must account for three potential arguments in the source and restructure to two arguments for the Snowflake UDF.  For Teradata, First argument can be a date or timestamp, second is optional for the calendar type,  third argument is optional and can be a time.
DocRef:
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.TD_MONTH_BEGIN_UDF(INPUT_DT TIMESTAMP_LTZ, CAL_TYPE VARCHAR(20))
RETURNS TIMESTAMP
AS
$$
SELECT TIMESTAMP_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(CALENDAR_DATE), MONTH_OF_YEAR, DAY_OF_MONTH, HOUR(INPUT_DT), MINUTE(INPUT_DT), SECOND(INPUT_DT), EXTRACT(NANOSECOND FROM CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)) 
FROM TDBUSINESSCALENDAR WHERE MONTHBEGIN = CALENDAR_DATE AND MONTH_OF_CALENDAR = 
     (SELECT MONTH_OF_CALENDAR - 1 FROM TDBUSINESSCALENDAR WHERE CALENDAR_TYPE = CAL_TYPE AND CALENDAR_DATE = DATE_TRUNC(DAY,INPUT_DT))
$$
;

RETURNS TIMESTAMP

$$

RETURNS TIMESTAMP

$$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.TD_WEEK_OF_MONTH_UDF(INPUT_1 TIMESTAMP_LTZ)
RETURNS INTEGER
AS
$$
SELECT TRUNC((DAYOFWEEK(DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT_1),MONTH(INPUT_1),1)) - 1 + DAYOFMONTH(INPUT_1))/7)
$$
;

Comment: Function derives value from the SYS_CALENDER.CALENDAR table.
DocRef:
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.TD_WEEK_OF_YEAR_UDF(INPUT_1 TIMESTAMP_LTZ)
RETURNS INTEGER
AS
$$
SELECT
    CASE WHEN WEEKISO(DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT_1),1,1)) = 53 THEN
        CASE WHEN WEEKISO(INPUT_1) = 53 AND DAYOFWEEK(INPUT_1) = 0 THEN 1
                WHEN WEEKISO(INPUT_1) = 53 THEN 0
                WHEN DAYOFWEEK(INPUT_1) = 0 THEN WEEKISO(INPUT_1) +1
                ELSE WEEKISO(INPUT_1)
        END
        WHEN WEEKISO(DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT_1),1,1)) = 52 THEN
            CASE WHEN WEEKISO(INPUT_1) = 52 AND MONTH(INPUT_1) = 1 AND DAYOFWEEK(INPUT_1) > 0 THEN 0
                WHEN WEEKISO(INPUT_1) = 52 AND MONTH(INPUT_1) = 1 AND DAYOFWEEK(INPUT_1) = 0 THEN 1
                WHEN DAYOFWEEK(INPUT_1) = 0 THEN WEEKISO(INPUT_1) + 1
            ELSE WEEKISO(INPUT_1)
        END
        ELSE 
            CASE WHEN MONTH(INPUT_1) = 12 AND WEEKISO(INPUT_1) = 1 THEN 52
                WHEN MONTH(INPUT_1) = 1 AND WEEKISO(INPUT_1) = 52 THEN 0
                WHEN DAYOFWEEK(INPUT_1) IN (0) THEN WEEKISO(INPUT_1) 
            ELSE WEEKISO(INPUT_1)-1
        END
    END
$$
;

Comment: Default format string is 'DD' if omitted in the source code.
DocRef:
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION SNOWCONVERT.PUBLIC.TRUNC_DATE_UDF(INPUT TIMESTAMP_LTZ, FMT VARCHAR(5))
RETURNS DATE
AS
$$
CAST(CASE 
WHEN FMT IN ('CC','SCC') THEN DATE_FROM_PARTS(CAST(LEFT(CAST(YEAR(INPUT) as CHAR(4)),2) || '00' as INTEGER),1,1)
WHEN FMT IN ('SYYYY','YYYY','YEAR','SYEAR','YYY','YY','Y') THEN DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1)
WHEN FMT IN ('IYYY','IYY','IY','I') THEN 
    CASE DAYOFWEEK(DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
         WHEN 0 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 1, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
         WHEN 1 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 0, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
         WHEN 2 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -1, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
         WHEN 3 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -2, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
         WHEN 4 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -3, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
         WHEN 5 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 3, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
         WHEN 6 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 2, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
    END        
WHEN FMT IN ('MONTH','MON','MM','RM') THEN DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),MONTH(INPUT),1)
WHEN FMT IN ('Q') THEN DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),(QUARTER(INPUT)-1)*3+1,1)
WHEN FMT IN ('WW') THEN DATEADD(DAY, 0-MOD(TIMESTAMPDIFF(DAY,DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1),INPUT),7), INPUT)
WHEN FMT IN ('IW') THEN DATEADD(DAY, 0-MOD(TIMESTAMPDIFF(DAY,(CASE DAYOFWEEK(DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                                 WHEN 0 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 1, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                                 WHEN 1 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 0, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                                 WHEN 2 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -1, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                                 WHEN 3 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -2, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                                 WHEN 4 THEN DATEADD(DAY, -3, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                                 WHEN 5 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 3, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                                 WHEN 6 THEN DATEADD(DAY, 2, DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),1,1))
                                                               END),      INPUT),7), INPUT)
WHEN FMT IN ('W') THEN DATEADD(DAY, 0-MOD(TIMESTAMPDIFF(DAY,DATE_FROM_PARTS(YEAR(INPUT),MONTH(INPUT),1),INPUT),7), INPUT)                                                             
WHEN FMT IN ('DDD', 'DD','J') THEN INPUT
WHEN 'D' IN ('DAY', 'DY','D') THEN DATEADD(DAY, 0-DAYOFWEEK(INPUT), INPUT)
WHEN FMT IN ('HH', 'HH12','HH24') THEN INPUT
WHEN FMT IN ('MI') THEN INPUT
END AS DATE)
$$
;

Snowflake Function: Teradata-proprietary function, not available in other RDBMS. Put windowing aggregate in WITH clause (CTE), then apply QUALIFY filter in query.
Comment: SNOW-35904
Customer Comments: Requires rewriting many statements, although simple to do with a code conversion tool.
DocRef:
-- Top3 Tables Scanned by Account using QUALIFY
SELECT
    ACCOUNT_ID
,   TABLE_ID
,   TABLE_NAME
,   SUM(ROWS_SCANNED) as SUM_ROWS
FROM
    VA.JOB_TABLE_ACCESSES
WHERE
    MONTH(CREATED_ON)   =   MONTH(CURRENT_DATE) -   1
AND ROWS_SCANNED        >   100000000
AND ACCOUNT_ID          IN  (16929, 7257, 865, 473, 136, 168) 
QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER(
        PARTITION BY
            ACCOUNT_ID
        ORDER BY
            SUM_ROWS    DESC
    )   <=  3
GROUP BY ACCOUNT_ID, TABLE_ID, TABLE_NAME
;

Evaluates a list of conditions and returns the position of the first condition that evaluates to TRUE, provided that no prior condition in the list evaluates to UNKNOWN.
Works with Periods, Derived Periods and DateTime expressions.  Periods are being translated to varchars.  This will take a complex UDF at a minimum to support.

Category: SELECT statement syntax, options, clauses, modifiers

Comment: Returns the number of n-gram matches between string1 and string2.
Extracts the value of a name-value pair where the name in the pair matches the name and the number of the occurrence specified.

A Teradata queue table is a FIFO data structure with a set of operations implemented as SQL extensions. The tables could be converted into Snowflake tables, but they would require some special extra columns. I think the operations could be implemented as UDFs/stored procedures. A proper answer would require more research. I would try to build a prototype, preferably based on a customer example and then based on the prototype a generic conversion strategy could be developed. In short they require solutioning as you alluded to it.

Comment: Enables users to specify whether the values of any fields associated with Identity Data are returned in response to a SQL Insert operation
Defines (creates or replaces) an SQL stored procedure in the database using an SPL input file.
Indicates what the precision should be for decimal values associated with subsequently issued SQL requests for non-Field Mode responses.
Restores initial default settings for commands used to control :report mode output format response data format import process message verbosity SQL request retries
Specifies if a copy of the Teradata SQL requests and BTEQ commands should be placed in the standard output stream.
Toggles request-level Full-Stream Encryption ON or OFF.
Assigns severity levels to database errors
Routes the standard error stream and the standard output stream to two files or devices for mainframe-attached systems, or to one file or device for workstation-attached client systems.
Logs off all Teradata Database sessions and then exits BTEQ.
Establishes the maximum time duration within which to allow a submitted SQL request to be processed before CLI will time out the request. Workstation platforms only.

Comment: Enhancement Requested
Specifies the name and format of an export file that BTEQ uses to store records resulting from data-returning SQL requests.
Toggles suppression of the additional Page Advance ASA Carriage Control Character at the top of a REPORT format export file when using z/OS BTEQ.
Splits each line of a report into two or more lines after the specified columns.
Specifies the footer text string that appears at the bottom of each page of a report.

Enables all page-oriented formatting commands, or disables them and centers the tabular report created for the returned rows, using the value of the WIDTH command option to determine the space available.
Specifies which escape sequence is to be used for format control of printed reports.
Enables a four-digit year format in header, footer, and title lines.
Skips over all intervening BTEQ commands and SQL statements until a specified label is encountered, then resumes processing in sequence.
Causes BTEQ to pause for a specified period of time.
Specifies a header that appears at the top of each page of a report.
Returns a list of all available BTEQ commands and their syntax. The SQL Online Help feature includes similar help for SQL commands.

Category: System Performance Monitor and Production Control (PMPC) APIs

Comment: Tests the validity of the condition stated in the IF clause.
Opens a supplied file, of the specified format, to process record data for USING modifiers of subsequent SQL requests.

Specifies Indicator Mode is to be used to return data rows from the Teradata Database in response to SQL data-returning requests. The data format includes indicator bit bytes for identifying null values.
Identifies the point at which BTEQ resumes processing, as specified in a previous GOTO command.
Enables the use of the Teradata Database Multipart Indicator response mode. This can help for the retrieval of Large Data Objects (LDOs), such as CLOB, BLOB, JSON, and XML data types.
Enables specification of the security mechanism and other parameters (beyond userid and password) needed to authenticate using during logons.
Enables specification of the security mechanism and other parameters (beyond userid and password) needed to authenticate using during logons.
Terminates the user's Teradata Database sessions without exiting from BTEQ.
Connects a user to a Teradata Database.
Enables bypass of prompts related to conventional LOGON command use.
Designates a maximum error severity level beyond which BTEQ terminates job processing.
Specifies the name of a file for BTEQ to store the messages that would normally be sent to the standard output.

Comment: Performs a user exit or pre-defined action when certain significant events occur.
Specifies a character or a character string to represent a null field value for Field Mode reports.
Returns the number of elements in array_expr that contain either a NULL or non-NULL value.
Removes all elements from array_expr.
Returns an integer value indicating whether the element specified by the index in array_expr contains a value or is in an uninitialized state.
Allocates space for one or more new elements in array_expr.
Returns the lowest subscript value in array_expr as either an unsigned INTEGER value or a new instance of the predefined ARRAY type ArrayVec.
Returns the highest subscript value with a populated element in array_expr.
Returns the highest possible subscript value in array_expr as either an unsigned INTEGER value or a new instance of the predefined ARRAY type ArrayVec.
Excludes specified columns from Field Mode reports for data-returning SQL statements.

Category: SELECT statement syntax, options, clauses, modifiers

Comment: Returns the subscript of the element subsequent to the element specified for array_expr, if that element is currently populated.
Returns the subscript of the element prior to the element specified for array_expr, if that element is currently populated.
Submits an operating system command to a workstation-attached system.
Removes one or more populated elements from the end of array_expr.
Enables sending multiple import file records with an SQL request.
Enables page ejection whenever the value for one or more specified columns changes.
Specifies the maximum number of lines on a report page.
Limits BTEQ's verbosity to errors and request processing statistics.
Specifies the maximum record length for the current session. This affects imports, exports, and stdout hex-dump output.

Specifies Record Mode is to be used to return data rows from the Teradata Database in response to SQL data-returning requests. Each row is presented in the same representation as it was returned in by the database, as specified by the SQL, using byte ordering appropriate for the client system. Null values, data types and data lengths are implicit.
Places a specified string on the standard output stream.
Submits the next Teradata SQL request a specified number of times.
Indicates whether a REPEAT operation should terminate if an error occurs for a request that indicates the request cannot be re-tried.
Determines the spacing used for Unicode® report mode output.
Cancels a request when the value specified for rows by the RETLIMIT command is exceeded.
Specifies the maximum number of rows and columns displayed on the terminal or written to a file in response to a Teradata SQL statement.
Specifies whether requests that fail under certain error conditions should be resubmitted.
Specifies a title which automatically includes the date and page number that appears at the top of each page of a report.
Processes the Teradata SQL requests and BTEQ commands from the specified run file.

Toggles issue of warnings in response to syntax errors in accordance with FIPS Publications 127-2 and 127-3 for ANSI compliance.
